title: Daily Briefing — Jun 30
headline: June swoon in full effect: defensive rotation, rising sector risk, and Fear & Greed stuck in the basement.
body: Right, so we've got classic midterm-year 'June swoon' playing out — R521/R529 are flashing bright red here. SPX is clinging to the SMA20 at 741, but breadth looks rubbish and Fear & Greed is stuck at 27.2 — that's proper fear territory. Seasonality says this is the drawdown window, not a contrarian buy-the-dip moment.

Sector rotation is telling the story: XLK (Tech) is 'weakening' with RS 120.5, and Energy (XLE) and Materials (XLB) both sitting at elevated and high risk levels, respectively. Defensive moves in XLP (Staples, RS 91.6) and XLY (Discretionary, RS 95.8) are improving, but most sectors are lagging. This is exactly how R521 and R529 describe the midterm-year hiccup — sector risk rises, and tech exhausts just as defensives rotate higher.

Contrarians: Don't fall for the bounce. Retail F&G is low, but that's NOT an opportunity per the rules — record sector risk and weak leadership trump sentiment here. QQQ, XLK, and SPY are at mean reversion levels; failed closes below their 20 MA = exit longs. If you're fishing for mean reversion, wait for the Q4 setup — cycles within cycles, guys.
callout_body:
SPY: 741 (SMA20) — close below triggers exit per rule
QQQ: 723.49 (SMA20) — watch daily close
XLK: 186.80 (SMA20) — trigger zone
XLE: 55.81 (SMA20) — risk rising
Fear & Greed: 27.2 — stuck on 'fear', but not contrarian buy per R521/R529
VIX: 17.65 — below SMA20 but watch for July/Aug seasonal spike per R498
Energy, Financials, Materials, Industrials: HIGH/ELEVATED risk — avoid chasing sector bounces
action_items: Reduce any tech overweight: XLK, QQQ, AAPL, MSFT sit below 20 MA — rule triggers apply.
If SPY or XLK close below their daily SMA20, trim risk — less trades = more wins, react don't predict.
closing_quote: Patience, react, don't predict — June swoons and midterm hiccups are cycles within cycles. Next trade!
